Narrative_Meaning: Sevens signify surprises, whether in the sense of conspiracy, illnesses and light hearted fun.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The card of vehicles, engines and powerful motivations. The Drive is about speed, motion and pressing forwards, regardless of dangers or costs. Sometimes these motivations to move are internal to a Character, and they may be driven by Faith or Doubts, other times they are external, being driven on by some force beyond their control.

Significator.Plot: The Drive Plot is usually a journey or odyssey. Sometimes the Vehicle is important, becoming a Character (often a Rival), other times it is barely mentioned. The Journey though is always more important than the destination.
Significator.Side: Drive Plots can affect individual sides of a Conflict, forcing them to move to find what they seek, or indicate that they have drive and will get closer to what they are working towards.
Significator.Character:
The Drive Significator indicates a Character who loves to drive (or pilot).

They Gain Yin when they talk about vehicles, motors, engines, riding animals.

Gain Yang when they look after, or maintain a vehicle or riding animal.

Gain Chi when they drive or ride a vehicle or riding animal.

They also gain a +1 Success Level when they are driving a vehicle or riding a riding animal.
Significator.Scene: The Drive Scene is usually a car chase or race Ordeal, but it could be a quiet drive in the countryside, or a quick flit on a plane somewhere. Travel by vehicle is suggested and usually with a soupçon of action and danger.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Geometry Yang Gain, whatever is required to make a Yang Gain is offered by the Hook.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Conflict opens with Action, usually a Motional Ordeal like a Car Chase, or Race.
Yarn.Gain: A Vehicle (A Vehicle Descendant) whether it is a run-down pick-up truck, a luxury yacht, or a Void-runner Space-liner.
Yarn.Fray_Description: An Opposed Motional Ordeal, usually of High or Extreme Stakes. This is usually a car chase or race (although it may be a Flee! situation). There are ample opportunities for Stunts and Obstacles during this Ordeal and all drivers are expected to complete them.
Yarn.Snag_Description: There is a traffic accident, or crash. Maybe someone runs into the PCs or they barrel into someone else. Either way Draw 5 Ordeal Cards and apply one each to each Driver, their Vehicles, and one for any bystanders/passengers (that usually Splashes). These Wounds may be soaked by safety systems and the Characters armour/Incarna as appropriate. If no vehicles are involved then you may Draw 3 and play one card on each 'Driver' who in this case is just running.

NPC.Description: The Jockey is a professional vehicle or riding animal fan. They love the things they drive and ride, spending hours cleaning, grooming, tweaking, and modifying their rides. They drive (or ride) the vehicles (or animals) they love for a living, whether trucks, jet-fighters or thoroughbred horses.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: When a Character needs to travel a long way, they can get a ride, and arrive fresh and rested in a new location. Getting a Ride will sometimes result in a Gain or Revelation, but is mostly about travel and rest.
Yarn.History_Description: The Drive indicates that either vehicular travel or some powerful motivations lie in the past of the situation. Did someone travel from far away in the past? Or has someone been driven to create this situation?
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Drive indicates a situation that is careening out of control, it may be right on the verge of disaster. Often this is a case of the characters not paying proper attention to the situation.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Drive indicates that the way out of the current situation is to take control of the situation and guide it towards a different destination than it may have been headed, or at least to ease on the brakes so everything doesn't fly off the rails.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about the background, or setting, not the current plot or situation. It might include pertinent details for the current plot, but is usually just about a reasonably common vehicle or mode of transport in the world. This might be information about a horse, a cart, a car, a mono-cycle, a starship, a time-ship, or a teleport booth
Revelations.Info_Description: Information about a Descendant, that may help or be hindering.
Revelations.Vector_Description: The information is found in a manual, or similar technical document. It should be an owner's manual, accounting ledgers, a treatise, a science paper, or perhaps a map or log-book, rather than an encyclopaedia that contains this information.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The Alternative information reveals where someone or something has been. Where have they been and who they have met can tell you a lot about a person or thing.
Revelations.Detail_Description: The detail is a piece of common knowledge that should be known to everyone in the world. Careful with the Narration that you give this Revelation, it can easily become "As you know, Bob" exposition, instead consider a child singing a nursery rhyme about it, or a local curse that includes the information, "He's gone like King Samand's marbles".

Obstacle.Description: The way is blocked, by fast paced, flowing traffic.

Tarot.Crossed: Instantly Fix or replace a Prop (Physical Descendant).
Tarot.Normal: Target drops a random Prop (Physical Descendant).

Ordeal.Stage_Description: A sloping route, or stairs down. A low spot or day.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Act quickly, and get through the watery Stages to the goal as fast as you, on your surfboard / giant-seahorse can.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Flowing Characters side press on the Ebbing Characters, trying to force them back by strength and weight of numbers. Ebbing Side Attack with -1 Pips on each card.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
A single Monster as powerful as the whole Party, or a Group with each member at least as powerful as the most powerful PC.
  Facet    Monster  
Awe ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Representing the creative even spiritual aspects of materialism such as beauty and art, that transcend base materialism like wealth. Despite this the Diamonds are recognised as being the Coins suit of the Tarot, with a strong tie to Elemental Earth.

Tarot Meaning: Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.

Parry:Seize the initiativeThe cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Terror — The Monster is a Fear or Anxiety embodied. They are quite diverse in form, but are always able to cause Psych attacks and Negative Psych effects to any that see them act. They may add Awe Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cause negative emotions in another Character. They automatically draw and play Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) cards as a Psych Attack on any Character that sees them make any attack. They must take Awe Draw cards from their Monster Pool to defend, Soak or Heal any Attack made upon them. Examples of this Monster include Ghosts, some serial killers and some Preternatural Cosmic Horrors.
Craft ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Maker — The Monster is actually a creator of other Monsters. The spawn are often less powerful than the creator, but in some cases are unique and more powerful. The Creator may not be directly encountered until late in the Story. The Creator can draw Craft Draw cards and add them to its Monster Pool each time it creates or summons an Extra or Descendant. At least 1 card must be used from the Monster Pool each Summoning or Creation to help pay the Summoning costs (or as an Obstacle / NPC Yarn card). All currently summoned Extras and Descendants receive a bonus to their Annex Scores and card Pips equal to the Creator’s Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced).
Orthodox ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Tyrant — The Orthodox Monster is a tyrant, often a part of “the system” (in that it acts as a method of control over a population). A Tyrant monster add Orthodox Draw cards to their Monster Pool every time they are cruel, ruthless, authoritarian or demanding. Tyrants can add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels on another Character’s actions if they order them to do something. Tyrants can also add Monster Pool Size Score Orthodox Failure Levels to an enemy action for a cost of one card from their Monster Pool. The Tyrant must discard half of their their Monster Pool if they are ignored, forgotten or scorned.
Quiet ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Hush — The Quiet Monster is exactly that, almost (or entirely) silent. It is the creeping death of a hidden illness, or the cat-footed predator striking without warning that vanishes without trace. Hush Monsters get to begin with Quiet Score cards in their Monster Pool. Hushes may add +1 Score / Pip for each card in their Monster Pool when they try to act silently. When a Hush creature makes a noise (often a scream or howl, including a Psych attack), it must play at least half of the Monster Pool on the action. Hush Monsters may add cards to their Monster Pool based on how long it has been since the last time they made a noise as a Yarn Extend Duration (e.g. After being silent for a few seconds add a card, after a scene of silence add two. Be silent for a week and add 6 cards to the Pool). Creatures like giant owls, snakes or cats, that stalk and hunt silently are good examples, as is a really creepy, floating mime. Many ghosts are also silent, until they turn and scream to create a Duck attack.
Virtue ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Friendly — The Monster is a “Friendly” monster, that will almost certainly be misunderstood and attacked by some people. They may add Virtue Draw cards to their Monster Pool by being kind to another, or whenever they are attacked. A friendly Monster protects their friends from harm, and must play cards from their Monster Pool to defend, protect, or heal them. Friendly monsters may add a number of Failure Levels equal to the Monster Pool Size Reduced to any enemy that tries to target them or their friends. Friendly Monsters can add Virtue Score (rolled Die or otherwise) to any positive action when playing a card from their Monster Pool. Examples might include a cynical, but big-hearted ogre; a being created from stitched together corpses; a dark-elf ranger with a unicorn necklace; or perhaps even an Electric Mouse.
Zeal ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Celestial — The Monster is a Celestial, a creature of divine or supernatural nature, often associated with a particular divinity, occasionally a political association, they often act as heralds of greater powers. Celestial beings include Anthropomorphic Personifications of social constructs like, Death, War, and Wyrd herself. When acting according to their nature, following their belief system, performing their duties, or following orders, they may draw Zeal Draw Ordeal cards into their Monster Pool. They automatically add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any action to do with their nature, duty, travel, or communication. They also add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to actions specific to their nature and duty. Celestial Monsters can only manifest a physical incarnation by playing cards directly from their Monster Pool on all physical actions. The monster could be an angel, a devil, a djinn, or perhaps even one of the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Example: Rat / Chicken / Crow / Piranha / Small Turtles
Lea.Notes: These paths often migrate long distances between two small territories.

Stress.Description: Stress makes things harder. Things that we could previously bear with grace become impossible to cope with, and all too often the Stress interferes with our skills and talents. Stress can even make us revel in these hardships, justifying and internalising the failures.
Stress.Rules: All Diamond suited Actions (Awe, Craft, Orthodox, Quiet, Virtue, Zeal) add Difficulty equal to the Character’s current Stress in Intrepid Facet Sway. If a penalised Action fails the Character may reduce Stress or Strain by 1 per Success Level.

Trauma.Description: Sometimes when Trauma hits, the only thing the Character feels is an urge to escape. This urge can drive them to leave town, change jobs or schools, or even leave their home planet or time.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma creates an urge to leave a place. The Size of the Location that the Character must move outside of is equal to the total Stress (as Chi). If the Character moves that far they may discard that Stress, so a Character with only 1 Stress must make only a single step, but a Character with 140 Stress would be leaving the continent. Time Travellers can insteaed escape through time, with Extend Duration defining how far they must move in time.

Age.Description: An Age of Vehicles is occurring. During this age a society gains access to a new method of travel, this opens up new vistas of trade and exploration for the society, granting them additional freedoms and responsibilities. Examples from history include the "Age of Oars", "Age of horse", "Age of Sail", "Age of Steam", "Automobile Age", "Jet Age" and "Space Age". Although technically every new vehicle creates its own age, many are short-lived events for that society, where as others can continue for a very lonng time.
Age.Rules: Access to a new vehicle or mode of transport within the society will have dramatic impacts upon that society. Additional freedom of movement, speed of travel, cheapness of trade and so on will positively affect the society, although responsibilities such as rail-road or fleet building can cost a society in the short-term the eventual benefits usually outweigh the costs.
